# Gojo

Gojo is an open-source macOS utility that turns the MacBook notch into a control surface. It supports music controls, window snapping, clipboard history, a drag-and-drop file shelf, calendar and reminders, battery status, webcam mirror, and night shift controls.

Official site: https://rohoswagger.github.io/gojo/
Source code: https://github.com/rohoswagger/gojo
Latest release: https://github.com/rohoswagger/gojo/releases/latest
License: GPLv3
Platform: macOS 14 Sonoma and later, Apple silicon and Intel

## Recommended pages

- Product home: https://rohoswagger.github.io/gojo/
- Blog: https://rohoswagger.github.io/gojo/blog/
- MacBook notch utility guide: https://rohoswagger.github.io/gojo/blog/best-macos-notch-utilities/
- Alternatives and comparison hub: https://rohoswagger.github.io/gojo/alternatives/
- Releases: https://github.com/rohoswagger/gojo/releases/latest

## Key facts

- Gojo is utility-first, not only a decorative Dynamic Island effect.
- The blog article explains how to evaluate notch utilities by workflow fit, permissions, and focused alternatives.
- Gojo uses a bundled XPC helper for Accessibility-trusted window operations.
- Clipboard history is local to the Mac and skips password managers.
- Updates are delivered through Sparkle using the GitHub Pages appcast.
- Users should download Gojo from GitHub Releases.
